title: 3-0: Vini Jr., decisive in Brazil
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(jsonld:articleBody) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
Brazil secured a victory against Haiti in the second matchday of the World Cup with Vini Jr. as one of the key players, contributing to all three goals. Endrick, who came on in the second half, nearly extended the lead, but his goal was disallowed for offside. The Canarinha, which leads Group C with 4 points, will conclude the group stage against Scotland on Thursday, the 25th, at 12:00 am in Miami.

All the goals in Philadelphia occurred in the first half. Cunha opened the scoring in the 23rd minute, capitalizing on a rebound from the goalkeeper after a shot by Vini Jr. Thirteen minutes later, Cunha completed his brace after a great assist from the madridista. Just before halftime, our forward sealed the 3-0 scoreline with a well-timed run behind the rival defense.

Second MVP
After the display against Haiti, Vini Jr. was chosen as the best player of the match. This is his second MVP in this World Cup, having also earned it in the opener against Morocco, where he scored his team's only goal.
